note: Objective answer key. All verdicts below were computed from the SHIPPED theme JSON files (sources/theme_candidates/*.json) by references/reference_analysis.py using standard WCAG 2.x relative-luminance contrast, Machado-2009 colorblind simulation matrices, and CIE76 deltaE. The pool is offline and frozen, so the answer is deterministic and never goes stale. The numbers below are reproducible by running reference_analysis.py against the pool.

hard_constraints.HC1_editor_contrast: editor.foreground on editor.background WCAG contrast >= 7.0 (AAA for normal text)
hard_constraints.HC2_meaning_token_contrast: every MEANING syntax token (keyword/string/function/variable/constant) has contrast >= 3.0 on editor.background (comments are exempt — conventionally dim)
hard_constraints.HC3_red_green_separation: keyword color vs string color must stay distinguishable for red-green colorblindness: CIE76 deltaE >= 20 under BOTH deuteranopia and protanopia simulation

soft_constraints.SC1_max_colorblind_separation: prefer the larger worst-case (min of deut/prot) keyword<->string deltaE — more robust under colorblindness
soft_constraints.SC2_max_min_meaning_contrast: prefer the higher minimum meaning-token contrast — more readable overall

per_candidate_results.trap: MOST POPULAR (657k downloads) and attractive, but editor fg/bg contrast is below AAA — the popularity/beauty trap

fails: HC3 (keyword and string share the SAME color, deltaE 0)
per_candidate_results.trap: monochrome-ish: keyword and string are the same hue → invisible distinction even for normal vision

fails: HC2/HC3 (default theme ships almost no tokenColors → no semantic syntax differentiation to evaluate)
per_candidate_results.trap: high base contrast but no syntax token colors → cannot satisfy semantic differentiation
highest contrast of all, but keyword==string==white → zero red/green separation

tiebreak.reason: Among the two hard-constraint survivors, Nord wins BOTH soft constraints: higher worst-case colorblind keyword<->string separation (43.5 > 41.3) AND higher minimum meaning-token contrast (4.64 > 3.93).

gold.acceptable_alternative: monokai (the only other hard-constraint survivor); selecting monokai with an explicit, correct tradeoff explanation may earn up to ~0.85 but is not the optimal pick

key_facts_executor_must_get_right.one_dark_pro_is_a_trap: must NOT be selected; it fails HC1 despite being the most popular/pretty
key_facts_executor_must_get_right.tomorrow_night_blue_and_kimbie_trap: highest contrast but keyword==string → fail red/green separation; must be rejected

scoring_anchors.tolerance_note: Executors may compute slightly different absolute numbers depending on the exact colorblind-simulation matrix / deltaE variant they use; grade on the ORDINAL outcome (which themes pass HC1/HC2/HC3, who the survivors are, and that nord >= monokai on the soft criteria), not on exact decimals. The qualitative verdicts above are robust across standard methods.
